sn.s: In de Schwiiz git's Reglä, wo niemmer ufschribt.
sn.e: In Switzerland there are rules nobody writes down.

sn.s: Aber alli haltet sich dra, ganz sälbverständlich.
sn.e: But everyone follows them, as a matter of course.

sn.s: Pünktlichkeit isch heilig. Z'spät cho isch unhöflich.
sn.e: Punctuality is sacred. Being late is rude.

sn.s: Wenn öpper "am Achti" seit, denn meint er Achti.
sn.e: When someone says "at eight", they mean eight.

sn.s: Am Sunntig isch Rueh. Mer wäscht nöd und bohrt nöd.
sn.e: On Sundays it's quiet. You don't do laundry or drilling.

sn.s: Au am Aabig nach Zähni isch Rueh aagseit.
sn.e: In the evening after ten, quiet is also expected.

sn.s: Mini Nachberin het mer das früeh erklärt.
sn.e: My neighbour explained that to me early on.

sn.s: "Mer macht kei Lärm, wenn anderi welled schlafe."
sn.e: "You don't make noise when others want to sleep."

sn.s: Abfall trännt mer suuber: Glas, Papier, Kompost.
sn.e: You separate rubbish neatly: glass, paper, compost.

sn.s: Wer das nöd macht, fallt schnäll uf.
sn.e: Whoever doesn't do that quickly stands out.

sn.s: Am Aafang han ich es paar Fähler gmacht.
sn.e: At the start I made a few mistakes.

sn.s: Aber d'Lüt sind gduldig, wenn mer's lärne wott.
sn.e: But people are patient when you want to learn.

sn.s: Die Reglä mached s'Zämeläbe eifacher für alli.
sn.e: These rules make living together easier for everyone.

sn.s: Wer sie respektiert, ghört schnäller derzue.
sn.e: Whoever respects them belongs more quickly.
